---
title: "CV Explorations"
subtitle: "A hands-on, benchmark-driven tour of modern computer vision"
---

This is a **repo-as-curriculum**: each major computer-vision capability is a
self-contained module that is both *runnable* and *explained*. Every module ships
a reusable CLI/engine, an interactive notebook, and a **rigorous benchmark report**
— multiple models per task, real metrics, latency measured on the local GPU, and
honest failure cases.

The reports you're reading were rendered from results computed locally on an
**NVIDIA RTX 3090 Ti (24 GB)**; heavier or Linux-only work runs on an **NVIDIA
DGX Spark** (ARM64 / GB10 Grace-Blackwell). Site rendering itself needs no GPU.
The same code is validated on both x86 and ARM64 — see
[Cross-platform validation](cross-platform.qmd).

## The curriculum

| # | Module | What it answers | Models benchmarked | Status |
|---|--------|-----------------|--------------------|--------|
| 01 | [**Object detection**](modules/01-object-detection/report.qmd) | *What & where (boxes)?* | RF-DETR · YOLO26 · YOLO11 · Grounding DINO | ✅ done |
| 02 | [Classification + zero-shot](modules/02-classification/report.qmd) | *What is this image?* | ConvNeXt · SigLIP 2 · OpenCLIP | ✅ done |
| 03 | [Segmentation](modules/03-segmentation/report.qmd) | *Which pixels (masks)?* | YOLO-seg · SAM 2.1 | ✅ done |
| 04 | [Monocular depth](modules/04-depth/report.qmd) | *How far is each pixel?* | Depth Anything V2 (S/B) | ✅ done |
| 05 | [Pose / keypoints](modules/05-pose/report.qmd) | *Where are the joints?* | YOLO26-pose · YOLO11-pose | ✅ done |
| 06 | [Tracking](modules/06-tracking/report.qmd) | *Same object across frames?* | YOLO26 + ByteTrack | ✅ done |
| 07 | [Embeddings / retrieval](modules/07-retrieval/report.qmd) | *How similar are two images?* | DINOv2 · SigLIP 2 | ✅ done |
| 08 | [Vision-language models](modules/08-vlm/report.qmd) | *Describe / answer / ground* | Qwen2.5-VL 3B | ✅ done |
| 09 | [Classical CV](modules/09-classical/report.qmd) | *(pre-deep-learning)* | OpenCV: edges, flow, ORB | ✅ done |
| 10 | [**Fine-tuning** (on the Spark)](modules/10-finetuning/report.qmd) | *Teach a model a new class* | YOLO11 · auto-labeled via CLIP + Grounding DINO | ✅ done |

## A useful mental model

The biggest shift in modern CV is **task-specific models → promptable foundation
models**:

- **Old way:** train one model per task on a fixed label set.
- **New way:** one model you *prompt* — SAM ("segment whatever I point at"),
  Grounding DINO ("detect anything I name in text"), CLIP ("classify against any
  labels"), Florence-2 ("detect, caption, OCR, and ground from one checkpoint").

Each module shows both, side by side, so the *why* of the modern approach is
visible rather than asserted.

## How to run it yourself

```bash
uv sync --group dev                # core env (PyTorch + CV stack)
uv sync --group data --group detection   # extras for the detection module
uv run python modules/01-object-detection/run.py --help
```

See the repository [README](https://github.com/chaoticcuriosity-io/cv-explorations)
for the full setup and hardware notes.
